pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage introducing the children’s ministry nonprofit, pulling organizational overview and featured content, and providing clear paths into events, giving, resources, shop, and getting involved.
primary_functions: Introduce the ministry’s mission, focus on Christian children’s discipleship, and highlight key programs.
Provide quick entry points to Events, Donate, Resources, Shop, Volunteer, and Child Sponsorship sections.
Feature timely highlights such as Summer Bible Camp, current lesson series, or sponsorship campaigns.
Surface shortcuts to parent tools like crafts, lesson plans, and the parent newsletter signup.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Events" link in the main navigation or a featured event/camp CTA card.
Click the "Donate" button in the header navigation or homepage giving CTA.
Click a homepage shortcut to crafts or activities for families.
Click the "Parent Newsletter" link in the footer or a homepage newsletter promo.

access_methods.description: Main landing page linked from the logo and the "Home" header nav item.
Users can land directly on the homepage via the root site URL.

pages.name: Events Listing
pages.description: Central hub to browse, search, and filter all events such as Summer Bible Camp, family events, and trainings, using structured filter and sort options backed by event APIs.
primary_functions: Browse a list or grid of upcoming events with key details like title, date, age range, location, and price.
Filter events by event type (e.g., Summer Bible Camp), date range, age range, price, location, and session type.
Sort events by price, date, or relevance (including 'Price: Low to High').
Open individual event detail pages by clicking an event card.

incoming_params.description: Pre-selected event type filter (e.g., 'summer_bible_camp') when deep-linking from homepage or campaigns.
ISO date string representing the start of the date range filter (e.g., '2025-07-01').
ISO date string representing the end of the date range filter (e.g., '2025-07-31').
Lower bound for age range filter when linking directly into age-specific events.
Upper bound for age range filter when linking directly into age-specific events.
Maximum per-child price filter (e.g., 200 for affordable camp search).
Postal/ZIP code used to pre-filter events by location radius.
Radius in miles for location-based filtering (e.g., 10).
Pre-selected session type filter value (e.g., 'morning').
Initial sort option key (e.g., 'price_asc' for 'Price: Low to High').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an event or camp card in the filtered results list.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Events" main navigation item.
Filters can be pre-configured via query parameters for campaigns and deep links.

pages.name: Event Detail & Registration
pages.description: Detailed view of a specific event or camp, including schedule, pricing, and on-page registration integrated with event details and session selections.
primary_functions: Display full event information including description, dates, times, age eligibility, location, and per-child cost.
Show available sessions (e.g., Morning Session) and capacity information if needed.
Provide a registration form that allows entry of multiple children with their ages and selected sessions.
Offer payment options such as 'Pay at Check-In' and capture parent/guardian contact details.
Submit registration and show an on-page confirmation message for the event.

incoming_params.description: Event ID for which to display details and provide registration.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to Events" link or breadcrumb from the event detail page.
Click a "Return Home" link shown after successful registration confirmation.

access_methods.description: Accessed when an event card is clicked, passing the event ID as a query parameter.
Featured event links on the homepage may deep-link directly using the event ID.

pages.name: Donate
pages.description: Donation page for one-time and recurring gifts to ministry funds like Children’s Bible Story Kits, configured by donation options interface and creating pledges via the donation API.
primary_functions: Allow donors to switch between one-time and monthly (recurring) donation options using tabs.
Offer preset giving amounts (including $35) and allow custom entry of other amounts.
Let donors designate their gift to specific funds such as 'Children's Bible Story Kits'.
Provide an option for donors to cover processing fees by checking a box.
Enable dedication of gifts (e.g., in honor or memory) with honoree name and message fields.
Collect donor information including name, email, and mailing address.
Provide payment method options including 'Bill me by mail' or 'Send me instructions to pay later' without requiring card details.
Submit and confirm the donation or monthly pledge with a clear acknowledgment message.

incoming_params.description: Pre-selects the donation tab, e.g., 'monthly' when coming from a monthly campaign.
Optional preset amount to pre-fill the donation (e.g., 35).
ID of a specific fund to be pre-selected (e.g., Children's Bible Story Kits).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to Home" or similar link on the thank-you/confirmation state of the page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the prominent "Donate" button in the header.
Linked from various calls-to-action across the site, such as fund spotlights and sponsorship pages.

pages.name: Crafts & Activities
pages.description: Resource library of printable and digital crafts, games, and activities for children’s ministry and families, with robust filtering and saving into personal collections.
primary_functions: Display a grid or list of craft and activity cards with titles, age ranges, themes (e.g., Easter), and estimated duration.
Filter activities by age range (e.g., 6–8), topic or season (e.g., Easter), difficulty, and duration (including maximum time of 30 minutes).
Sort results by different criteria such as 'Most Downloaded', newest, or most liked.
Provide a 'Save' or 'Add to Favorites' button/icon on each craft card to add it to My Saved Resources for the current user session.
Open detailed craft/activity pages or quick views if needed (while keeping the main template focused on browsing and saving).

incoming_params.description: Lower bound for pre-selected age filter, e.g., 6.
Upper bound for pre-selected age filter, e.g., 8.
Pre-selected seasonal topic filter, e.g., 'easter'.
Maximum duration in minutes for activity duration filter (e.g., 30).
Initial sort option, e.g., 'most_downloaded'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "My Saved Resources" header link or a "View Saved" call-to-action after saving items.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Crafts & Activities" item in the main navigation.
Linked from the homepage and other resource-oriented CTAs.
Supports pre-filtered deep links for specific ages, topics, and durations.

pages.name: Saved Resources & Collections
pages.description: User’s personal workspace for viewing saved crafts and lesson plans, organizing them into named collections, and accessing those collections for planning and reuse.
primary_functions: Display a list of all resources (e.g., crafts, activities, lesson plans) that the user has saved or favorited.
Allow creation of new collections with a custom name (e.g., 'Easter 6–8 Crafts').
Enable selection of multiple saved resources via checkboxes to bulk-add them to a chosen collection.
Provide an 'Add to Collection' dropdown or control to assign selected items to a specific collection.
List all collections in a sidebar or panel and allow the user to open a collection by clicking its name.
Show all resources within a selected collection in a focused view so the user can see the grouped activities together.
Support renaming or deleting collections if needed for organization (optional, but aligned with user needs).

incoming_params.description: ID of the collection that should be opened/selected by default when the page loads.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a collection name in the sidebar to open that collection view.
Click a "Find more crafts & activities" button from the Saved Resources page.
Click a "Browse lesson plans" link near saved lesson resources.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "My Saved Resources" item in the header.
Specific collections can be opened directly using a collectionId query parameter.
Linked from save confirmations and resource pages after saving items.

pages.name: Lesson Plans & Series Creation
pages.description: Lesson plan library with filters and tools to create lesson series for specific age groups and calendar months, while also supporting saving of individual plans.
primary_functions: Display lesson plans focused on biblical topics (e.g., Teachings of Jesus > Parables) with age range and duration details.
Filter lesson plans by age group (e.g., Ages 5–7), topic (including nested topics like 'Parables'), scripture focus, and complexity.
Provide a calendar or date filter control to select a month (e.g., April 2025) and show its four Sundays for planning.
Allow users to create a new lesson series by entering a series name (e.g., 'April 2025 Parables (Ages 5–7)').
Offer an 'Add to Series' action on each lesson plan card to add that lesson to an existing series.
Show a dropdown of existing series in the 'Add to Series' popup for the user to choose where to place the lesson.
Confirm addition of lessons to a series and visually indicate which lessons are already part of a given series.

incoming_params.description: Lower age bound for lesson plan age filter, e.g., 5.
Upper age bound for lesson plan age filter, e.g., 7.
Pre-selected high-level topic category, e.g., 'teachings_of_jesus'.
Pre-selected subtopic, e.g., 'parables'.
Month for the calendar view (1–12), e.g., 4 for April 2025 planning.
Year for the calendar view, e.g., 2025.
ID of an existing series to prioritize in 'Add to Series' dropdowns.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "View all series" or "Series Planner" link.
After creating a new series, click "Open in Planner" for that series.
Click a "View My Saved Resources" link from within the lesson plan library.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Lesson Plans" item in the main navigation.
Supports pre-filtered access by age, topic, and calendar month for planning flows.

pages.name: Series Overview
pages.description: Dashboard view listing all lesson series the user has created for different age groups and time periods, interacting with series APIs to list and create series.
primary_functions: List all existing lesson series with key information such as name, age group, month, and number of weeks.
Allow users to select and open a specific series (e.g., 'April 2025 Parables (Ages 5–7)') to manage its lessons.
Provide options to create additional series or duplicate an existing one for another age group or date range.
Offer quick links back to the lesson plan library for adding more lessons to a selected series.

incoming_params.description: Optional month filter to focus the series list on a specific month.
Optional year filter to focus the series list on a specific year.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a series card or "Manage" button for that series.
Click "Add more lessons" from a series row to open the lesson library pre-filtered.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Series Planner" item in the header navigation.
Supports optional filtering by month and year via query parameters.

pages.name: Series Detail
pages.description: Detailed planner page for a single lesson series, allowing week-by-week arrangement and saving of lesson order, integrating calendar context and drag-and-drop ordering via series APIs.
primary_functions: Display all lessons that have been added to the selected series, grouped by planned week (e.g., Week 1–4).
Allow users to drag and drop lessons to reorder them across weeks (e.g., arrange four parables lessons into Weeks 1–4).
Provide a 'Save Series Order' button that stores the current arrangement of lessons for that series.
Show the calendar context (e.g., the four Sundays in April 2025) to ensure lessons are scheduled appropriately.
Optionally allow notes for each week (e.g., service details or volunteer reminders) to support planning (not required for core tasks but supportive).

incoming_params.description: Lesson series ID to load for detailed week-by-week arrangement.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to all series" link or breadcrumb.
Click "Add more lessons" from within the series detail planner.

access_methods.description: Reached when a specific series is selected from the Series Overview or after creating a series.

pages.name: Shop / Product Listing
pages.description: Online shop for curriculum sets, printed materials, and other ministry resources with filtering, search, and category navigation backed by product APIs.
primary_functions: Display a product grid listing items like curriculum sets with thumbnail image, title, price, and age range.
Allow browsing by category via a sidebar (e.g., 'Curriculum Sets') and other categories as needed.
Provide a search bar to enter keywords such as 'Fruit of the Spirit' and view matching products.
Offer filters for age groups (e.g., Ages 3–5, Ages 9–11), price range (including a maximum of $25), and other attributes.
Allow sorting of products by criteria such as 'Customer Rating: High to Low', price, or newest.
Open individual product detail pages when a product card is clicked.

incoming_params.description: Pre-selected product category (e.g., Curriculum Sets) for the sidebar filter.
Search keyword to pre-populate the search box (e.g., 'Fruit of the Spirit').
Lower age bound for filtering products by age suitability.
Upper age bound for filtering products by age suitability.
Maximum price filter, e.g., 25 for affordable curriculum searches.
Sort key, e.g., 'rating_desc' for 'Customer Rating: High to Low'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a product card in the shop grid.
Click the cart icon in the header or a "View Cart" link from the shop page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Shop" link in the main navigation.
Supports deep links with pre-selected category, search query, and filters.

pages.name: Product Detail
pages.description: Detailed product page for a specific resource or curriculum set, including purchase options and add-to-cart integration.
primary_functions: Show comprehensive product information including description, age range, scope and sequence, included components, and pricing.
Display customer ratings and reviews where applicable to support sorting and selection decisions.
Provide an 'Add to Cart' button to add the current product to the shopping cart.
Offer quantity selection or format options if applicable (e.g., print vs. digital).
Link back to the previous product listing or search results to continue shopping.

incoming_params.description: Product ID for which to display details and allow adding to cart.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the cart icon or a "View Cart" link after adding an item.
Click a "Back to Shop" or "Continue Shopping" link.

access_methods.description: Accessed by clicking a product from the Shop listing with the product ID in the query string.

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Shopping cart page summarizing items the user intends to purchase, including curriculum sets, backed by cart interfaces to fetch and update contents.
primary_functions: Display all items added to the cart with product name, age group, price, quantity, and subtotal.
Allow users to update quantities or remove items from the cart.
Show cart totals including any estimated taxes or shipping if applicable.
Provide options to proceed to a checkout or payment instructions flow (even if payment is later completed offline).
Offer links back to the shop to continue browsing and adding more products.

incoming_params.description: Optional indicator of source page (e.g., 'shop') to tailor back-navigation messaging.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Continue Shopping" link or button.
Click a "Also support the ministry" donation CTA from the cart page (if shown).

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Cart" icon/link in the header navigation.
Linked after adding products to the cart from product detail pages.

pages.name: Volunteer Opportunities Listing
pages.description: Listing page for all volunteer opportunities, including in-person child-focused Sunday morning roles, with filters for serve type, role focus, location, and availability.
primary_functions: Display volunteer opportunities with titles, campus or site, role focus, time commitments, and distance from a given location.
Filter opportunities by serve type (e.g., In-Person), role focus (e.g., Work Directly with Children), and other criteria.
Allow users to enter a ZIP code (e.g., 30309) and distance (e.g., within 10 miles) to filter by location.
Provide availability filters for day of the week (e.g., Sunday) and time slots (e.g., Morning 8am–12pm).
Sort opportunities by distance or other relevance metrics.
Open detailed volunteer opportunity pages when a listing card is clicked.

incoming_params.description: Pre-selected serve type filter, e.g., 'in_person'.
Pre-selected role focus, e.g., 'work_directly_with_children'.
ZIP/postal code filter, e.g., '30309'.
Radius filter in miles, e.g., 10.
Selected day of week, e.g., 'sunday'.
Selected time block, e.g., 'morning_8_12'.
Sort key, e.g., 'distance_asc'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a volunteer opportunity card in the list.

access_methods.description: Accessible from the "Volunteer" option in the main navigation.
Supports deep-link filters for campaigns targeting specific volunteer types.

pages.name: Volunteer Opportunity Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific volunteer role with on-page application form, integrating opportunity details and submission of applications.
primary_functions: Present full description of the volunteer role including responsibilities, age group served, training, and background check requirements.
Show location details, campus or site information, and available service times (e.g., Sunday 9:00am service).
Provide a 'Sign Up to Volunteer' call-to-action that scrolls to or reveals the application form.
Collect applicant information including full name, email address, phone number, and campus selection if applicable.
Allow applicants to indicate specific availability (e.g., selecting a particular Sunday morning service time).
Submit the volunteer application and show a confirmation or next steps message.

incoming_params.description: Volunteer opportunity ID to load details and allow application submission.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to Opportunities" link or breadcrumb.
Click a "Return Home" link after submitting the application and viewing confirmation.

access_methods.description: Accessed by selecting an opportunity from the Volunteer Opportunities listing.

pages.name: Child Sponsorship Listing
pages.description: Gallery of children available for sponsorship with filters for monthly amount, age, and interests, backed by child sponsorship search APIs.
primary_functions: Display child sponsorship profiles in a grid or list with name (or identifier), age, country/region, and key interests.
Allow filtering by monthly sponsorship amount range (e.g., $30–$40) using a slider or input fields.
Provide age range filters (e.g., 6–9 years) and interest tags such as 'Reading & Bible Stories'.
Enable sorting of profiles by criteria such as 'Longest Waiting' so that children waiting the longest appear first.
Open an individual child profile page when a card is clicked to view more details and start sponsorship.

incoming_params.description: Minimum monthly sponsorship amount filter, e.g., 30.
Maximum monthly sponsorship amount filter, e.g., 40.
Minimum child age filter, e.g., 6.
Maximum child age filter, e.g., 9.
Interest tag to filter profiles, e.g., 'reading_and_bible_stories'.
Sort key, e.g., 'longest_waiting'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a child sponsorship profile card.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Child Sponsorship" header navigation link.
Supports deep-link filters for campaigns highlighting specific age or interest groups.

pages.name: Child Profile & Sponsorship
pages.description: Profile page for an individual child where users can start a monthly sponsorship, using sponsorship creation APIs for billing preferences and start dates.
primary_functions: Display child information including age, background story, interests, and sponsorship impact details.
Show available sponsorship amounts within the filtered range (e.g., 'Sponsor for $35/month').
Provide a sponsorship form to capture sponsor’s full name, email address, and other basic contact information.
Offer a date picker for the sponsor to choose a sponsorship start date (e.g., the first day of the next month).
Present payment options including 'Bill me by mail' or 'Invoice by mail' without requiring online card entry.
Confirm sponsorship setup after submission with clear next steps and mailing expectations.

incoming_params.description: Child profile ID for which to display details and allow sponsorship setup.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to all children" link above or below the profile.

access_methods.description: Accessed when a child card is selected from the Child Sponsorship listing.

pages.name: Parent Newsletter Signup
pages.description: Signup page for parents to subscribe to targeted newsletters with age-specific family devotion content, using newsletter option and subscription APIs.
primary_functions: Collect parent email address for newsletter subscription (e.g., parent@example.com).
Allow parents to select their children's ages via multi-select or checkboxes (e.g., 8, 9, and 10).
Offer topic preferences such as 'Family Devotions' and 'Helping Kids Pray'.
Provide an email frequency dropdown with options including 'Weekly'.
Allow parents to set communication preferences (e.g., 'Email only') and request age-specific ideas for selected ages.
Submit the newsletter signup and show a confirmation that preferences have been saved.

incoming_params.description: Optional pre-filled parent email address, if known or passed from a promo link.
Lower bound of children’s ages to pre-select for age-specific ideas.
Upper bound of children’s ages to pre-select for age-specific ideas.

outgoing_connections.trigger: After successful signup, click a "Back to Home" link or button from the confirmation state.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Parent Newsletter" link in the site footer.
Linked from homepage or resource pages encouraging newsletter signup.

pages.name: About / Contact / Policies
pages.description: Informational page describing the ministry’s mission and beliefs, with basic contact information, contact form, and key website policies loaded via organization and policy APIs.
primary_functions: Explain the organization’s mission, Christian beliefs, and approach to children’s ministry and family support.
Provide contact details (e.g., mailing address, phone number, email) and optionally a simple contact form for general inquiries.
Summarize or link to privacy, data use, and terms of use information related to the website.

incoming_params.description: Which tab/section to focus on when loading, e.g., 'about', 'contact', or 'policies'.
Optional pre-selected reason for contact (e.g., 'volunteering', 'donations').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Give Now" or support CTA from the About page.
Click a "Serve with us" or volunteer CTA from the About page.
Click a "Back to Home" link after reading about/contact/policies content.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "About", "Contact", and "Privacy & Terms" footer links (with differing section anchors/params).
Can be linked directly in emails or external communications as the About/Contact/Policies landing page.

header_links.description: Homepage with ministry overview and quick access to key sections.
Browse and filter events such as Summer Bible Camp and family programs.
Access lesson plans and create lesson series for different age groups.
Browse crafts and activities resources for at-church and at-home use.
Purchase curriculum sets and other ministry resources.
Find and sign up for volunteer opportunities in children’s ministry.
View children available for sponsorship and start a monthly pledge.
Make a one-time or monthly donation to support ministry programs.
View and organize your saved crafts, activities, and lesson plans.
Manage your lesson series and access detailed series planners.
View and manage items added to your shopping cart.

footer_links.description: Learn about the ministry’s mission and background.
Find contact information or send a general inquiry.
Read about website privacy practices and terms of use.
Subscribe to the parent newsletter for family devotion ideas.
Find craft ideas and activities for kids.
Visit the resource shop to purchase materials.
Serve in children’s ministry through volunteer roles.
Sponsor a child and support their spiritual growth.
Support the ministry financially with a gift or pledge.
